purpose: Catalog public Lattice UI evidence and convert it into preliminary Zorn UI/proto requirements.

confidence_scale.high: Official Anduril docs or screenshots tied directly to official developer workflows.
confidence_scale.medium_high: Official company news/exercise imagery with visible UI but limited documentation context.
confidence_scale.medium: Partner articles or sanitized portfolio material.
confidence_scale.low: Public social screenshots; supporting evidence only.

sources.title: Anduril developer docs — Watch entities
sources.notes: Official docs page for getting an entity from the UI and streaming entity events. Includes Lattice UI asset details imagery.
key_claims: The Lattice UI has an Entity Explorer and an Entity ID column.
SDK consumers can GetEntity and StreamEntities for real-time entity component updates.

sources.title: Anduril developer docs — Sample apps
sources.notes: Official sample app overview. Includes entity visualizer, objects CLI, AIS integration, entity thumbnail, and task-an-asset samples.
key_claims: The entity visualizer renders all entities in an environment on a map.
Sample screenshots show tasking and bearing/range visualization.

sources.title: Anduril developer docs — Entities overview
sources.notes: Official data-model source for entities, templates, lifecycle, assets, tracks, and geo-entities.
key_claims: Entities power the common operating picture.
Entities are component-based and may be partial in real time.
UI may group entities by ontology component.
Asset, Track, and Geo-entity are the three top-level shapes.

sources.title: Anduril developer docs — Tasks overview
sources.notes: Official source for task model, task catalog, task status, and task lifecycle semantics.
key_claims: Tasks model deliberate sequential actions an operator can execute on an asset or team of assets.
Taskable agents can be services, assets, groups, or humans.
Task catalog advertises task definitions an agent can perform.
Task Manager routes, persists, monitors, and maintains task state across the COP.

sources.title: Anduril developer docs — Update tasks
sources.notes: Official task status update guide with screenshot evidence of task selection/execution UI.
key_claims: Task status represents lifecycle stage.
Agents report real-time status updates with UpdateStatus.

sources.title: Anduril developer docs — Objects overview
sources.notes: Official object-store source for file/data storage, object lifecycle, CDN/mesh behavior, and entity/object use cases.
key_claims: Objects represent a single file or piece of data.
Objects can be used with Entities for track thumbnails and vessel manifests.
Object lifecycle includes create, expire, and delete.

sources.title: Lauren Park portfolio — Anduril Lattice UI
sources.notes: Sanitized designer portfolio describing Lattice Live COP, object multi-selection, grouping, bulk editing, and starring.
key_claims: Lattice Live COP provides a real-time battlespace overview by integrating sensors into one interface.
Object multi-selection supports shift-click from object cards and dragging over map objects.
Multi-selection panel, refine-selection filters, bulk metadata editing, grouping, ungrouping, confirmation snackbar, and starred panel are described.

sources.title: Wind River blog — Lattice Sandbox integration
sources.notes: Partner blog about early Lattice Sandbox access and integration with Lattice SDK. Includes sandbox UI screenshots with simulated assets and tasks.
key_claims: Lattice C2/mesh integration imports telemetry such as lat/lon, heading, altitude, velocity, and sensor readings.
Screenshots show asset details, payloads, data overrides, task dropdown, and task execute/discard workflow.

sources.title: Anduril news — Desert Guardian 1.0
sources.notes: Public exercise screenshot showing satellite map, hostile air vehicle tracks, loiter/engagement regions, and Roadrunner task cards.
key_claims: Operational UI includes layers/search/analytics/map options, active threat tracks, mission zones, and asset task cards.

sources.title: Hartpunkt article — Anduril Lattice selected for U.S. Army IBCS-M
sources.notes: German article with Anduril-attributed C-UAS/fire-control UI screenshot. Use as supporting visual evidence only.
key_claims: Image shows defended areas, sensor cones, hostile tracks, triage states, pending/current/complete task columns, and emergency stop controls.

sources.title: Public social screenshot — Lattice Live drone tracking
sources.notes: Unverified public screenshot of Lattice Live showing wardragon-detection track details. Keep as low-confidence supporting evidence.
key_claims: Image shows selected map track with inline telemetry popup and matching detail fields.

observed_ui: Actions menu: Follow, Manual, Anchor to Map Center, Copy Content, Manage Asset
Copy Asset URL and Copy Asset Location submenu

observed_ui: Entity Explorer table/list implied by official docs
Entity ID column for copy/use in SDK calls

observed_ui: Bottom map scale and cursor coordinate display

images.title: Task assignment workflow on satellite map
Task dropdown from asset panel
observed_ui: Task list includes BDA, Follow, Investigate, Manual, Smack, Target
Task menu items include info/help icons
Payloads / Task Catalog / Tags sections visible

images.title: Object map visualization and object list
Multi-selection, bulk action, grouping, ungroup confirmation
Sandbox UAV task details interface
observed_ui: Blue bounded task/selection frame on map
Task Details panel for BDA-like mission

images.title: Sandbox simulated asset with payloads and data overrides
observed_ui: Payloads section with operational status and count
Data Overrides section with validation/check state

images.title: Desert Guardian operational C2 screenshot
observed_ui: Satellite map with hostile air tracks
Action buttons such as scuttle / RTB / loiter visible in public image summary

images.title: Public social Lattice Live drone tracking screenshot
observed_ui: Dark map with multiple drone/track markers
Left panel/details implied by selected route URL

requirements.title: Map-first Live COP shell
requirements.requirement: Zorn shall provide a map-first, dark-theme Live COP shell with live status, map toolbar, entity layers, and selected-object panels.
acceptance: An operator can open the COP and see a live map canvas with entity markers.
The UI shows live/disconnected state and at least one top toolbar row.
The UI can render both dark/vector-like and satellite basemap modes.

requirements.title: Global toolbar
requirements.requirement: Zorn should expose global map controls for layers, search, analytics, and map options.
acceptance: Toolbar includes layer visibility controls and search.
Analytics/map options can initially be stubs with disabled or placeholder states.

requirements.title: Entity Explorer
requirements.requirement: Zorn shall provide an Entity Explorer/Object list that supports browsing, selecting, searching, and sorting entities.
acceptance: Entity list includes name, template/type, disposition/status, and entity ID access.
Search filters by name and ID.
Sorting is available for at least name, status, and last update.

requirements.title: Copy entity ID
requirements.requirement: Zorn shall allow operators/developers to copy an entity ID from the Entity Explorer or detail panel.
acceptance: A copy action writes the entity ID to clipboard.
Copied entity ID matches the entity ID used by API GetEntity/StreamEvents.

requirements.title: Entity detail panel
requirements.requirement: Zorn shall show a detail panel for selected entities containing status, template/platform, disposition, data type, ID, simulation/live state, kinematics, location, timestamps, provenance, and health where present.
acceptance: Missing components render as 'No <field>' or similar, not as UI errors.
Fields update live when entity stream updates arrive.
Time since last update is visible and recalculates.

requirements.title: Expandable entity component sections
requirements.requirement: Zorn should group selected entity details into expandable sections such as Asset Data, Payloads, Data Overrides, Task Catalog, Tags, and Media.
acceptance: At least Asset Data and Task Catalog sections are supported in P0/P1.
Payloads and Data Overrides render if present.

requirements.title: Entity action menu
requirements.requirement: Zorn shall provide an entity action menu with Follow, Manual/control placeholder, Anchor to Map Center, Copy Content, and Manage Asset actions.
acceptance: Follow pins the camera or selection to the entity.
Anchor to Map Center opens/creates a map anchor state.
Manual and Manage Asset may be disabled until backed by Zorn control APIs.

requirements.title: Copy asset URL/location/content
requirements.requirement: Zorn should support copy actions for asset URL, asset location, and serialized content.
acceptance: Copy URL and location clipboard actions produce stable strings.
Copy Content produces a JSON representation of the effective entity view.

requirements.title: Inline map entity popup
requirements.requirement: Zorn should show a compact inline popup when hovering or selecting a map entity, repeating key details from the side panel.
acceptance: Popup shows name/ID, status, disposition, speed/heading/altitude/location, and last update when present.

requirements.title: Entity clustering
requirements.requirement: Zorn should cluster high-density entities and show count markers on the map.
acceptance: Clusters expand or zoom on click.
Cluster count matches number of contained entities.

requirements.title: Cursor coordinates and scale
requirements.requirement: Zorn shall show map scale and cursor coordinate/readout status.
acceptance: Bottom status bar includes cursor coordinate and scale or zoom reference.
Coordinate format can be configured later.

requirements.title: Live status bar
requirements.requirement: Zorn shall show a bottom or persistent status bar indicating live state, current/UTC time, and last event timing.
acceptance: Live/disconnected state is visible.
Last event received time is visible.

requirements.title: Task catalog-derived task menu
requirements.requirement: Zorn shall derive available task actions from the selected asset's task catalog where available, with fallbacks for sample fixtures.
acceptance: Task menu renders only capabilities advertised by the selected asset when taskCatalog is present.
Task definitions keep their type.googleapis.com URLs in the underlying model.

requirements.title: Task launcher dropdown
requirements.requirement: Zorn shall provide a task dropdown/action launcher from the selected asset detail panel.
acceptance: Operator can choose a task type from a selected asset.
Choosing a task opens task details or map-prompt mode.

requirements.title: Task details panel
requirements.requirement: Zorn shall provide a Task Details panel with task type, assigned asset(s), objective/target, parameters, conflict state, and execute/discard controls.
acceptance: Panel supports target selection from the map.
Panel supports execute and discard states.
Panel can show parameter fields such as standoff distance.

requirements.title: Create task from map selection
requirements.requirement: Zorn shall allow operators to create a task from an asset and a map-selected target or point.
acceptance: Create task stores description, specification, assignee relation, author, and initial entities where available.
Map target prompt is visible while target is required.

requirements.title: Execute/discard task workflow
requirements.requirement: Zorn shall support task draft, execute, discard, status update, complete, and cancel workflows.
acceptance: Task status starts with a sent/draft-like state and updates through executing/completed/cancelled states.
Status updates are visible to the operator without refreshing.

requirements.title: Task geometry overlays
requirements.requirement: Zorn shall render bearing/range lines and task-related geometry between assigned assets and objectives.
acceptance: Range and bearing are displayed for asset-to-target tasking.
Task geometry updates if asset or target location changes.

requirements.title: Task conflict prompt
requirements.requirement: Zorn should expose task conflict state and prompt operators to resolve conflicts before execution.
acceptance: Conflicting tasks show a warning/prompt.
Execute is blocked or clearly warned when conflict is unresolved.

requirements.title: Mission/task cards
requirements.requirement: Zorn should show active/pending/current/completed task cards with progress/readiness and context actions.
acceptance: Task card shows asset, task, status, progress/readiness, and available actions.
Cards can be grouped by pending/current/complete.

requirements.title: Geo-zone overlays
requirements.requirement: Zorn should render geo-entity shapes and task/mission zones such as loiter zones, corridors, defended areas, and polygons.
acceptance: Point, polygon, and ellipse/circle shapes can be rendered.
Zones can be associated with tasks or geo-entities.

requirements.title: Sensor coverage overlays
requirements.requirement: Zorn may render sensor cones/coverage sectors as map overlays.
acceptance: Coverage overlays are separately toggleable by layer.

requirements.title: Threat/disposition visualization
requirements.requirement: Zorn shall visualize disposition/threat using distinct map marker styles and detail-panel labels.
acceptance: Friendly, neutral, unknown/suspect/suspicious, and hostile states are visually distinguishable.
Disposition changes are reflected live when override/update events arrive.

requirements.title: Payload panel
requirements.requirement: Zorn should display payload components/statuses for assets when available.
acceptance: Payload count and operational status render if present.
Add Payload action may be placeholder until mutation semantics are defined.

requirements.title: Data overrides panel
requirements.requirement: Zorn should show and eventually manage field-level data overrides, including override status/validity.
acceptance: Existing overrides appear under selected entity.
Override source/provenance is preserved.

requirements.title: Objects panel
requirements.requirement: Zorn should provide an Objects panel for object/media records and entity-associated object references.
acceptance: Objects list shows path/id, size, last updated, expiry if available.
Object-to-entity media associations render in entity details.

requirements.title: Track thumbnails and manifests
requirements.requirement: Zorn should support rendering media/object references such as track thumbnails and vessel manifests through the entity media component.
acceptance: Thumbnail object path resolves to preview image if available.
Expired/deleted object references show a broken/unavailable state and can be cleared.

requirements.title: Multi-select entrypoints
requirements.requirement: Zorn should allow multi-selection through shift-click on object/entity cards and map-area selection.
acceptance: Shift-click adds/removes entity from selection set.
Box/lasso selection adds visible map entities to selection set.

requirements.title: Multi-selection panel
requirements.requirement: Zorn should provide a multi-selection panel with selected count, search, sort, filters, and clear/remove actions.
acceptance: Panel reflects current selection set.
Filters refine selected entities without losing underlying selection membership.

requirements.title: Bulk metadata edit
requirements.requirement: Zorn should support bulk metadata edit actions over a selection set.
acceptance: Bulk edit draft shows affected count.
Save emits a confirmation snackbar and records an operator action log.

requirements.title: Create and manage entity groups
requirements.requirement: Zorn should allow creating groups from selected entities and adding entities to existing groups.
acceptance: Group has ID, name, member entity IDs, creator, created time.
Groups can be selected/taskable later as agents where supported.

requirements.title: Ungroup confirmation
requirements.requirement: Zorn should require confirmation before ungrouping/removing a group.
acceptance: Destructive ungrouping uses a modal with cancel and confirm.
Confirmation snackbar appears after success.

requirements.title: Starred entities
requirements.requirement: Zorn should let operators star objects/entities from cards, details panels, and map context menus, then access them from a Starred panel.
acceptance: Star state persists per operator/session.
Starred panel lists all starred entities and enables quick selection/fly-to.

requirements.title: Operator feedback snackbar
requirements.requirement: Zorn should show confirmation/error snackbars after bulk edit, group add/remove, star, and task actions.
acceptance: Snackbar includes action result and optional undo where safe.

requirements.title: Threat engagement queue
requirements.requirement: Zorn may provide a pending/current/complete queue for C-UAS or fire-control workflows.
acceptance: Queue states are separable from generic task cards.
Emergency stop state is clearly visible when enabled.

requirements.title: Emergency stop / abort action affordance
requirements.requirement: Zorn may expose high-salience abort/emergency-stop controls for simulated engagements only.
acceptance: Safety actions require confirmation or privileged role.
Initial implementation is mock-only and does not control physical systems.

requirements.title: Remote ID / drone detection track fields
requirements.requirement: Zorn may provide specific rendering for Remote ID / small UAS detection fields such as serial number, ANSI/CTA ID, operator/home point, and wardragon-detection provenance.
acceptance: Remote ID fields render as metadata when present without needing dedicated schema in P0.

P2: Emergency stop/abort UI for simulated flows
